mnemonics: WordNet-Wise: WordNet is a lexical database of semantic relations between words, organized like a network.
CORPUS = Collection Of Real-world, Processed, Unified Sentences.
keyPoints: Lexical resources are databases or collections of information about words, such as definitions, synonyms, and parts of speech.
WordNet is a major lexical database for English, grouping words into sets of synonyms (synsets) and recording semantic relationships.
Other important resources include corpora (large collections of text) used for statistical analysis and training NLP models.
commonMistakes: Confusing a lexical database (like WordNet) with a simple dictionary; they contain rich semantic relationships beyond just definitions.
Assuming all words in a language are covered in a single resource; coverage varies and multiple resources are often needed.
Using a resource built for one domain (e.g., general English) on text from a very different domain (e.g., medical journals) without verifying its suitability.
